parameters.query:
-- V2.3: Fetch customer requirements WITH fallback to sessions table
-- Uses COALESCE to merge data from both tables
SELECT 
  COALESCE(cr.session_id, s.session_id) as session_id,
  COALESCE(cr.company_name, s.customer_company) as company_name,
  COALESCE(cr.service_required, s.contract_type) as service_required,
  COALESCE(cr.deal_value::text, s.budget_range_max::text, '500000') as deal_value,
  COALESCE(cr.number_of_bidders::text, s.number_of_bidders::text, '3') as number_of_bidders,
  COALESCE(cr.market_position, s.market_position, 'moderate') as market_position,
  COALESCE(cr.decision_timeline, s.decision_timeline, 'normal') as decision_timeline,
  COALESCE(cr.service_criticality, 'important') as service_criticality,
  COALESCE(cr.alternative_options, '') as alternative_options,
  COALESCE(cr.switching_costs, 'moderate') as switching_costs,
  COALESCE(cr.budget_flexibility, 'moderate') as budget_flexibility,
  COALESCE(cr.walk_away_point, '') as walk_away_point,
  COALESCE(cr.in_house_capability, 'partial') as in_house_capability,
  COALESCE(cr.incumbent_status, '') as incumbent_status,
  cr.strategic_answers,
  cr.questionnaire_data,
  cr.annual_revenue,
  CASE WHEN cr.session_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'customer_requirements' ELSE 'sessions_fallback' END as data_source
FROM sessions s
LEFT JOIN customer_requirements cr ON s.session_id = cr.session_id
WHERE s.session_id = '{{ $json.sessionId }}'::uuid;
SELECT * FROM provider_capabilities WHERE session_id = '{{ $('Extract Session ID').first().json.sessionId }}'::uuid

SELECT pb.*, s.session_number, s.status as session_status FROM provider_bids pb JOIN sessions s ON s.session_id = pb.session_id WHERE pb.session_id = '{{ $('Extract Session ID').first().json.sessionId }}'::uuid

INSERT INTO leverage_calculations (
  id,
  session_id,
  bid_id,
  leverage_type,
  customer_leverage,
  provider_leverage,
  alignment_percentage,
  is_aligned,
  leverage_factors_breakdown,
  calculated_at,
  version,
  calculation_trigger
) VALUES (
  gen_random_uuid(),
  '{{ $json.sessionId }}',
  '{{ $('Fetch Provider Bid & Questionnaire').first().json.bid_id }}',
  'master',
  {{ $json.customerLeverage }},
  {{ $json.providerLeverage }},
  0,
  false,
  '{{ $json.leverageFactorsBreakdown }}'::jsonb,
  NOW(),
  1,
  'questionnaire_complete'
)
ON CONFLICT (session_id, bid_id, leverage_type) DO UPDATE SET
  customer_leverage = EXCLUDED.customer_leverage,
  provider_leverage = EXCLUDED.provider_leverage,
  leverage_factors_breakdown = EXCLUDED.leverage_factors_breakdown,
  calculated_at = NOW(),
  version = leverage_calculations.version + 1
RETURNING id, session_id, bid_id, customer_leverage, provider_leverage

UPDATE sessions 
SET 
  status = 'negotiation_ready',
  customer_leverage = {{ $('Calculate Leverage').first().json.customerLeverage }},
  provider_leverage = {{ $('Calculate Leverage').first().json.providerLeverage }},
  leverage_tracker_customer = {{ $('Calculate Leverage').first().json.customerLeverage }},
  leverage_tracker_provider = {{ $('Calculate Leverage').first().json.providerLeverage }},
  leverage_calculated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
WHERE session_id = '{{ $('Calculate Leverage').first().json.sessionId }}'::uuid
RETURNING 
  session_id, 
  session_number, 
  status,
  customer_leverage,
  provider_leverage,
  leverage_tracker_customer,
  leverage_tracker_provider,
  leverage_calculated_at;

SELECT 
  s.session_id,
  s.status,
  s.customer_leverage,
  s.provider_leverage,
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM leverage_calculations WHERE session_id::text = s.session_id::text) as leverage_count,
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM session_clause_positions WHERE session_id = s.session_id) as clause_count,
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM session_clause_positions WHERE session_id = s.session_id AND clause_level = 0) as parent_count,
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM session_clause_positions WHERE session_id = s.session_id AND clause_level = 1) as child_count
FROM sessions s
WHERE s.session_id = '{{ $('Calculate Leverage').first().json.sessionId }}'::uuid;

parameters.content:
## V2.3 FIX

**Problem:** Workflow failed when customer_requirements was empty.

**Solution:** Modified Fetch Customer Requirements to JOIN with sessions table and use COALESCE for fallback values.

Now the query:
1. Always returns data (from sessions at minimum)
2. Uses customer_requirements when available
3. Falls back to sessions table data when not
4. Includes `data_source` field to track which table was used
